findings.evidence: `BaseChange.lean:126` has `universe u` and `variable {k K H R S : Type u} ... [CommRing H]`; `PointsFunctor.lean:44` defines points with `{R : Type u} ... {H : Type v} [Semiring H] [_root_.HopfAlgebra R H]`; Mathlib tensor base change also uses `Type*` and `[Semiring A]`, e.g. `HopfAlgebra/TensorProduct.lean` variables `[Semiring A]`.
findings.fix: Generalize this section to separate universes, as in `PointsFunctor.lean`, and replace `[CommRing H]` with the semiring/Hopf assumptions actually needed, e.g. `{k : Type u} {K : Type v} {H : Type w} {R S : Type x}` with `[Semiring H] [_root_.HopfAlgebra k H]`.
findings.issue: `baseChangePointsIso` requires `{k K H R S : Type u}` and `[CommRing H]`, so it does not cover the existing `HopfAlgebra.points` API for arbitrary Hopf algebras; the commutativity and same-universe assumptions are not part of the mathematical statement.

summary: The new group-level base-change API is semantically over-narrow: it advertises Hopf-algebra points but only works when the Hopf algebra and all value/base rings live in the same universe, and when the Hopf algebra is commutative.
